revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''甲屋敷古墳'''（こうやしきこふん）とは、愛知県小牧市にある[[古墳時代]]の[[古墳]]である。本格的な調査が行なわれ...'
revision.text:
'''甲屋敷古墳'''（こうやしきこふん）とは、愛知県小牧市にある[[古墳時代]]の[[古墳]]である。本格的な調査が行なわれていないためはっきりとしたことは解かっていないが、出土品から[[3世紀]]末から[[4世紀]]頃に建設されたと考えられている。また現在すでに3分の2ちかくが破壊されたと見られており、現状[[円墳]]のように見えるが、建設された当時は[[前方後方墳]]か[[前方後円墳]]であった可能性が考えられている。
古墳の大きさは高さが5mで直径30m。内部構造は[[粘土槨]]である。[[1978年]]（昭和53年）[[3月25日]]には出土品である[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が小牧市の有形[[文化財]]に、[[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）[[8月15日]]には古墳自体が小牧市の[[史跡]]に指定されている。
==歴史== *[[3世紀]]末～[[4世紀]]頃 - 古墳が建設される *[[昭和]]初期 - 古墳内から多量の赤い[[顔料]]とともに、[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が出土。 *[[1975年]]（昭和50年）頃 - 小牧市の歴史書編纂にともない、調査が実施される。埋葬主体の一部が発見される。 *[[1978年]]（昭和53年）[[3月25日]] - 三角縁神獣鏡が小牧市の有形[[文化財]]に指定される *[[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）[[8月15日]] - 古墳が小牧市の[[史跡]]に指定される
==出土品== *三角縁神獣鏡 - 昭和初期、古墳の土を削った際に見つかった。直径は20.8cmで、古墳時代に[[日本]]国内で製造されたと考えられている。1975年（昭和50年）頃に小牧市の歴史に関する書籍の編纂にともない古墳の調査が行なわれた際、埋葬主体の一部が発見されたが、この中に納められていたのではないかとも考えられている。現在鏡は[[小牧市歴史館]]に保存・展示されている。なお似たような鏡は、古墳の南側にある[[宇都宮神社_(小牧市)|宇都宮神社]]などからも出土している。 *赤い顔料
==所在地== 愛知県小牧市小木2丁目474番地1号
==交通手段== *[[名鉄バス]]の小木[[バス停留所|停留所]]下車、徒歩3分。
==周辺== *[[愛知県道25号春日井一宮線|道25号春日井一宮線]] *[[愛知県道158号小口名古屋線|県道158号小口名古屋線]] *[[愛知県道451号名古屋外環状線|県道451号名古屋外環状線]] *[[宇都宮神社_(小牧市)|宇都宮神社]] *浄音寺
==関連項目== *[[小木古墳群]] - この古墳を含むこの辺り一帯にある古墳の総称。
==外部リンク== *[http://202.222.35.82/contents/11103870.html 甲屋敷古墳] *[http://202.222.35.82/contents/11103880.html 甲屋敷古墳出土の三角縁神獣鏡] *[http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/contents/11103670.html 小木古墳群]
{{DEFAULTSORT:こうやしきこふん}} [[Category:愛知県の古墳]] [[Category:小牧市]]

==出土品== *三角縁神獣鏡 - 昭和初期、古墳の土を削った際に見つかった。直径は20.8cmで、古墳時代に[[日本]]国内で製造されたと考えられている。1975年（昭和50年）頃に小牧市の歴史に関する書籍の編纂にともない古墳の調査が行なわれた際、埋葬主体の一部が発見されたが、この中に納められていたのではないかとも考えられている。現在鏡は[[小牧市歴史館]]に保存・展示されている。なお似たような鏡は、古墳の南側にある[[宇都宮神社_(小牧市)|宇都宮神社]]などからも出土している。 *赤い顔料
==所在地== 愛知県小牧市小木2丁目474番地1号
==交通手段== *[[名鉄バス]]の小木[[バス停留所|停留所]]下車、徒歩3分。
==周辺== *[[愛知県道25号春日井一宮線|道25号春日井一宮線]] *[[愛知県道158号小口名古屋線|県道158号小口名古屋線]] *[[愛知県道451号名古屋外環状線|県道451号名古屋外環状線]] *[[宇都宮神社_(小牧市)|宇都宮神社]] *浄音寺

'''甲屋敷古墳'''（こうやしきこふん）とは、愛知県小牧市にある[[古墳時代]]の[[古墳]]である。本格的な調査が行なわれていないためはっきりとしたことは解かっていないが、出土品から[[3世紀]]末から[[4世紀]]頃に建設されたと考えられている。古墳の高さは約5m、直径約30m。現状[[円墳]]のように見えるが、現在すでに3分の2程が破壊されたと見られており、建設された当時は[[前方後方墳]]か[[前方後円墳]]であったのではないかと考えられている。内部構造は[[粘土槨]]。[[1978年]]3月には出土品である[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が小牧市の有形[[文化財]]に、また[[1992年]]8月には古墳全体が同市の[[史跡]]に指定されている。
==歴史== *[[3世紀]]末～[[4世紀]]頃 - 古墳が建設される *[[昭和]]初期 - 古墳内から多量の赤い[[顔料]]とともに、[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が出土。 *[[1975年]]（昭和50年）頃 - 小牧市の歴史書編纂にともない、調査が実施される。埋葬主体の一部が発見される。 *[[1978年]]（昭和53年）[[3月25日]] - 三角縁神獣鏡が小牧市の有形[[文化財]]に指定される *[[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）[[8月15日]] - 古墳が小牧市の[[史跡]]に指定される
==出土品== *三角縁神獣鏡 - 昭和初期、古墳の土を削った際に見つかった。直径は20.8cmで、古墳時代に[[日本]]国内で製造されたと考えられている。1975年（昭和50年）頃に小牧市の歴史に関する書籍の編纂にともない古墳の調査が行なわれた際、埋葬主体の一部が発見されたが、この中に納められていたのではないかとも考えられている。現在鏡は[[小牧市歴史館]]に保存・展示されている。<ref>似たような鏡は、古墳の南側にある[[宇都宮神社_(小牧市)|宇都宮神社]]などからも出土している。</ref> *赤い顔料
==所在地== 愛知県小牧市小木2丁目474番地1号
==交通手段== *[[名鉄バス]]の小木[[バス停留所|停留所]]下車、徒歩3分。
==周辺== *[[愛知県道25号春日井一宮線|県道25号春日井一宮線]] *[[愛知県道158号小口名古屋線|県道158号小口名古屋線]] *[[愛知県道451号名古屋外環状線|県道451号名古屋外環状線]] *[[宇都宮神社_(小牧市)|宇都宮神社]] *浄音寺

'''甲屋敷古墳'''（こうやしきこふん）とは、愛知県小牧市にある[[古墳時代]]の[[古墳]]である。本格的な調査が行なわれていないためはっきりとしたことは解かっていないが、出土品から[[3世紀]]末から[[4世紀]]頃に建設されたと考えられている。古墳の高さは約5m、直径約30m。現状[[円墳]]のように見えるが、現在すでに3分の2程が破壊されたと見られており、建設された当時は[[前方後方墳]]か[[前方後円墳]]であったのではないかと考えられている。内部構造は粘土槨<ref>棺などが粘土で覆われた構造のこと。</ref>。[[1978年]]3月には出土品である[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が小牧市の有形[[文化財]]に、また[[1992年]]8月には古墳全体が同市の[[史跡]]に指定されている。

==出土品== *三角縁神獣鏡 - 昭和初期、古墳の土を削った際に見つかった。直径は20.8cmで、古墳時代に[[日本]]国内で製造されたと考えられている。1975年（昭和50年）頃に小牧市の歴史に関する書籍の編纂にともない古墳の調査が行なわれた際、埋葬主体の一部が発見されたが、この中に納められていたのではないかとも考えられている。現在鏡は[[小牧市歴史館]]に保存・展示されている。<ref>似たような鏡は、古墳の南側にある[[宇都宮神社_(小牧市)|宇都宮神社]]などからも出土している。</ref> *赤い顔料

==交通手段== *[[名鉄バス]]の小木[[バス停留所|停留所]]下車、徒歩3分。
==周辺== *[[愛知県道25号春日井一宮線|県道25号春日井一宮線]] *[[愛知県道158号小口名古屋線|県道158号小口名古屋線]] *[[愛知県道451号名古屋外環状線|県道451号名古屋外環状線]] *[[宇都宮神社_(小牧市)|宇都宮神社]] *浄音寺

revision.text:
'''甲屋敷古墳'''（こうやしきこふん）とは、愛知県小牧市にある[[古墳時代]]の[[古墳]]である。本格的な調査が行なわれていないためはっきりとしたことは解かっていないが、出土品から[[3世紀]]末から[[4世紀]]頃に建設されたと考えられている。古墳の高さは約5m、直径約30m。現状[[円墳]]のように見えるが、現在すでに3分の2程が破壊されたと見られており、建設された当時は[[前方後方墳]]か[[前方後円墳]]であったのではないかと考えられている。内部構造は[[粘土槨]]<ref>棺などが粘土で覆われた構造のこと。</ref>。[[1978年]]3月には出土品である[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が小牧市の有形[[文化財]]に、また[[1992年]]8月には古墳全体が同市の[[史跡]]に指定されている。
==歴史== *[[3世紀]]末～[[4世紀]]頃 - 古墳が建設される *[[昭和]]初期 - 古墳内から多量の赤い[[顔料]]とともに、[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が出土。 *[[1975年]]（昭和50年）頃 - 小牧市の歴史書編纂にともない、調査が実施される。埋葬主体の一部が発見される。 *[[1978年]]（昭和53年）[[3月25日]] - 三角縁神獣鏡が小牧市の有形[[文化財]]に指定される *[[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）[[8月15日]] - 古墳が小牧市の[[史跡]]に指定される
==出土品== *三角縁神獣鏡 - 昭和初期、古墳の土を削った際に見つかった。直径は20.8cmで、古墳時代に[[日本]]国内で製造されたと考えられている。1975年（昭和50年）頃に小牧市の歴史に関する書籍の編纂にともない古墳の調査が行なわれた際、埋葬主体の一部が発見されたが、この中に納められていたのではないかとも考えられている。現在鏡は[[小牧市歴史館]]に保存・展示されている。<ref>似たような鏡は、古墳の南側にある[[宇都宮神社_(小牧市)|宇都宮神社]]などからも出土している。</ref> *赤い顔料
==所在地== 愛知県小牧市小木2丁目474番地1号
==交通手段== *[[名鉄バス]]の小木[[バス停留所|停留所]]下車、徒歩3分。
==周辺== *[[愛知県道25号春日井一宮線|県道25号春日井一宮線]] *[[愛知県道158号小口名古屋線|県道158号小口名古屋線]] *[[愛知県道451号名古屋外環状線|県道451号名古屋外環状線]] *[[宇都宮神社 (小牧市)|宇都宮神社]] *浄音寺
==脚注== <references />
==関連項目== *[[小木古墳群]] - この古墳を含むこの辺り一帯にある古墳の総称。
==外部リンク== *[http://202.222.35.82/contents/11103870.html 甲屋敷古墳] *[http://202.222.35.82/contents/11103880.html 甲屋敷古墳出土の三角縁神獣鏡] *[http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/contents/11103670.html 小木古墳群]
{{DEFAULTSORT:こうやしきこふん}} [[Category:愛知県の古墳]] [[Category:小牧市の歴史]]

'''甲屋敷古墳'''（こうやしきこふん）とは、愛知県小牧市にある[[古墳時代]]の[[古墳]]である。本格的な調査が行なわれていないためはっきりとしたことは解かっていないが、出土品から[[3世紀]]末から[[4世紀]]頃に建設されたと考えられている。古墳の高さは約5m、直径約30m。現状[[円墳]]のように見えるが、現在すでに3分の2程が破壊されたと見られており、建設された当時は[[前方後方墳]]か[[前方後円墳]]であったのではないかと考えられている。内部構造は[[粘土槨]]<ref>棺などが粘土で覆われた構造のこと。</ref>。[[1978年]]3月には出土品である[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が小牧市の有形[[文化財]]に<ref>{{Cite web |date=2012-09-06 |url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiyukei/002801.html |title=甲屋敷古墳出土の三角縁神獣鏡 |publisher=小牧市 |accessdate=2013-05-28}}</ref>、また[[1992年]]8月には古墳全体が同市の[[史跡]]に指定されている<ref>{{Cite web |date=2012-09-06 |url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiminzoku/002810.html |title=甲屋敷古墳 |publisher=小牧市 |accessdate=2013-05-28}}</ref>。
==歴史== *[[3世紀]]末～[[4世紀]]頃 - 古墳が建設される *[[昭和]]初期 - 古墳内から多量の赤い[[顔料]]とともに、[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が出土。 *[[1975年]]（昭和50年）頃 - 小牧市の歴史書編纂にともない、調査が実施される。埋葬主体の一部が発見される。 *[[1978年]]（昭和53年）[[3月25日]] - 三角縁神獣鏡が小牧市の有形[[文化財]]に指定される *[[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）[[8月15日]] - 古墳が小牧市の[[史跡]]に指定される
==出土品== *三角縁神獣鏡 - 昭和初期、古墳の土を削った際に見つかった。直径は20.8cmで、古墳時代に[[日本]]国内で製造されたと考えられている。1975年（昭和50年）頃に小牧市の歴史に関する書籍の編纂にともない古墳の調査が行なわれた際、埋葬主体の一部が発見されたが、この中に納められていたのではないかとも考えられている。現在鏡は[[小牧市歴史館]]に保存・展示されている。<ref>似たような鏡は、古墳の南側にある[[宇都宮神社_(小牧市)|宇都宮神社]]などからも出土している。</ref> *赤い顔料
==所在地== 愛知県小牧市小木2丁目474番地1号
==交通手段== *[[名鉄バス]]の小木[[バス停留所|停留所]]下車、徒歩3分。
==周辺== *[[愛知県道25号春日井一宮線|県道25号春日井一宮線]] *[[愛知県道158号小口名古屋線|県道158号小口名古屋線]] *[[愛知県道451号名古屋外環状線|県道451号名古屋外環状線]] *[[宇都宮神社 (小牧市)|宇都宮神社]] *浄音寺

==関連項目== *[[小木古墳群]] - この古墳を含むこの辺り一帯にある古墳の総称。
==外部リンク== *[http://202.222.35.82/contents/11103870.html 甲屋敷古墳] *[http://202.222.35.82/contents/11103880.html 甲屋敷古墳出土の三角縁神獣鏡] *[http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/contents/11103670.html 小木古墳群]

'''甲屋敷古墳'''（こうやしきこふん）とは、愛知県小牧市にある[[古墳時代]]の[[古墳]]である。本格的な調査が行なわれていないためはっきりとしたことは解かっていないが、出土品から[[3世紀]]末から[[4世紀]]頃に築造されたと考えられている。 ==概要== 古墳の高さは約5m、直径約30m。現状[[円墳]]のように見えるが、現在すでに3分の2程が破壊されたと見られており、築造された当時は[[前方後方墳]]か[[前方後円墳]]であったのではないかと考えられている。内部構造は[[粘土槨]]<ref>棺などが粘土で覆われた構造のこと。</ref>。[[1978年]]3月には出土品である[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が小牧市の有形[[文化財]]に<ref>{{Cite web |date=2012-09-06 |url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiyukei/002801.html |title=甲屋敷古墳出土の三角縁神獣鏡 |publisher=小牧市 |accessdate=2013-05-28}}</ref>、また[[1992年]]8月には古墳全体が同市の[[史跡]]に指定されている<ref>{{Cite web |date=2012-09-06 |url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiminzoku/002810.html |title=甲屋敷古墳 |publisher=小牧市 |accessdate=2013-05-28}}</ref>。

revision.text:
'''甲屋敷古墳'''（こうやしきこふん）とは、[[愛知県]][[小牧市]]にある[[古墳時代]]の[[古墳]]である。本格的な調査が行なわれていないためはっきりとしたことは解かっていないが、出土品から[[3世紀]]末から[[4世紀]]頃に築造されたと考えられている。 ==概要== 古墳の高さは約5m、直径約30m。現状[[円墳]]のように見えるが、現在すでに3分の2程が破壊されたと見られており、築造された当時は[[前方後方墳]]か[[前方後円墳]]であったのではないかと考えられている。内部構造は[[粘土槨]]<ref>棺などが粘土で覆われた構造のこと。</ref>。[[1978年]]3月には出土品である[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が小牧市の有形[[文化財]]に<ref>{{Cite web |date=2012-09-06 |url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiyukei/002801.html |title=甲屋敷古墳出土の三角縁神獣鏡 |publisher=小牧市 |accessdate=2013-05-28}}</ref>、また[[1992年]]8月には古墳全体が同市の[[史跡]]に指定されている<ref>{{Cite web |date=2012-09-06 |url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiminzoku/002810.html |title=甲屋敷古墳 |publisher=小牧市 |accessdate=2013-05-28}}</ref>。
==歴史== *[[3世紀]]末～[[4世紀]]頃 - 古墳が築造される *[[昭和]]初期 - 古墳内から多量の赤い[[顔料]]とともに、[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が出土。 *[[1975年]]（昭和50年）頃 - 小牧市の歴史書編纂にともない、調査が実施される。埋葬主体の一部が発見される。 *[[1978年]]（昭和53年）[[3月25日]] - 三角縁神獣鏡が小牧市の有形[[文化財]]に指定される *[[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）[[8月15日]] - 古墳が小牧市の[[史跡]]に指定される
==出土品== *三角縁神獣鏡 - 昭和初期、古墳の土を削った際に見つかった。直径は20.8cmで、古墳時代に[[日本]]国内で製造されたと考えられている。1975年（昭和50年）頃に小牧市の歴史に関する書籍の編纂にともない古墳の調査が行なわれた際、埋葬主体の一部が発見されたが、この中に納められていたのではないかとも考えられている。現在鏡は[[小牧市歴史館]]に保存・展示されている。<ref>似たような鏡は、古墳の南側にある[[宇都宮神社_(小牧市)|宇都宮神社]]などからも出土している。</ref> *赤い顔料
==所在地== 愛知県小牧市小木2丁目474番地1号
==交通手段== *[[名鉄バス]]の小木[[バス停留所|停留所]]下車、徒歩3分。
==周辺== *[[愛知県道25号春日井一宮線|県道25号春日井一宮線]] *[[愛知県道158号小口名古屋線|県道158号小口名古屋線]] *[[愛知県道451号名古屋外環状線|県道451号名古屋外環状線]] *[[宇都宮神社 (小牧市)|宇都宮神社]] *浄音寺
==脚注== <references />
==関連項目== *[[小木古墳群]] - この古墳を含むこの辺り一帯にある古墳の総称。
==外部リンク== *[http://202.222.35.82/contents/11103870.html 甲屋敷古墳] *[http://202.222.35.82/contents/11103880.html 甲屋敷古墳出土の三角縁神獣鏡] *[http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html 小木古墳群]
{{DEFAULTSORT:こうやしきこふん}} [[Category:愛知県の古墳]] [[Category:小牧市の歴史]] [[Category:愛知県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:愛知県内の市町村指定史跡]]

revision.text:
'''甲屋敷古墳'''（こうやしきこふん）とは、[[愛知県]][[小牧市]]にある[[古墳時代]]の[[古墳]]である。[[小木古墳群]]の1つ。
==概要== [[3世紀]]末～[[4世紀]]頃に築造されたと推定される。古墳の3分の2はすでに失われてる<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=甲屋敷古墳{{!}}小牧市|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html|website=www.city.komaki.aichi.jp|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>。[[昭和]]初期の土取作業宙に、古墳内から多量の赤い[[顔料]]と[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が出土した<ref name=":0" />。[[1975年]]（昭和50年）の小牧市市史編纂調査により、埋葬主体の一部が発見された<ref name=":0" />。[[1978年]]（昭和53年）[[3月25日]]、三角縁神獣鏡が小牧市の有形[[文化財]]に指定される<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=甲屋敷古墳出土の三角縁神獣鏡（小牧市歴史館に展示中）{{!}}小牧市|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html|website=www.city.komaki.aichi.jp|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>。[[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）[[8月15日]]、古墳が小牧市の[[史跡]]に指定された<ref name=":0" />。
==出土品== *三角縁神獣鏡 - 直径は20.8cmで、古墳時代に[[日本]]国内で製造されたと考えられている<ref name=":1" />。現在、[[小牧市歴史館]]に保存・展示されている<ref name=":1" />。 *赤い顔料
==所在地== 愛知県小牧市小木2丁目474番地1号
==交通手段== *[[名鉄バス]]の小木[[バス停留所|停留所]]下車、徒歩3分。
==脚注== <references />
==関連項目== *[[小木古墳群]] - この古墳を含むこの辺り一帯にある古墳の総称。
==外部リンク== *[http://202.222.35.82/contents/11103870.html 甲屋敷古墳] *[http://202.222.35.82/contents/11103880.html 甲屋敷古墳出土の三角縁神獣鏡] *[http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html 小木古墳群]
{{DEFAULTSORT:こうやしきこふん}} [[Category:愛知県の古墳]] [[Category:小牧市の歴史]] [[Category:愛知県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:愛知県内の市町村指定史跡]]

'''甲屋敷古墳'''（こうやしきこふん）とは、[[愛知県]][[小牧市]]にある[[古墳時代]]の[[古墳]]である。[[小木古墳群]]の1つ。
== 概要 == [[3世紀]]末～[[4世紀]]頃に築造されたと推定される。古墳の3分の2はすでに失われてる<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=甲屋敷古墳{{!}}小牧市|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html|website=www.city.komaki.aichi.jp|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>。[[昭和]]初期の土取作業宙に、古墳内から多量の赤い[[顔料]]と[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が出土した<ref name=":0" />。[[1975年]]（昭和50年）の小牧市市史編纂調査により、埋葬主体の一部が発見された<ref name=":0" />。[[1978年]]（昭和53年）[[3月25日]]、三角縁神獣鏡が小牧市の有形[[文化財]]に指定される<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=甲屋敷古墳出土の三角縁神獣鏡（小牧市歴史館に展示中）{{!}}小牧市|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html|website=www.city.komaki.aichi.jp|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>。[[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）[[8月15日]]、古墳が小牧市の[[史跡]]に指定された<ref name=":0" />。
== 出土品 == *三角縁神獣鏡 - 直径は20.8cmで、古墳時代に[[日本]]国内で製造されたと考えられている<ref name=":1" />。現在、[[小牧市歴史館]]に保存・展示されている<ref name=":1" />。 *赤い顔料
== 所在地 == 愛知県小牧市小木2丁目474番地1号
== 交通手段 == *[[名鉄バス]]の小木[[バス停留所|停留所]]下車、徒歩3分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references />
== 関連項目 == *[[小木古墳群]] - この古墳を含むこの辺り一帯にある古墳の総称。
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html 甲屋敷古墳] *[http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html 甲屋敷古墳出土の三角縁神獣鏡] *[http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html 小木古墳群]
{{DEFAULTSORT:こうやしきこふん}} [[Category:愛知県の古墳]] [[Category:小牧市の歴史]] [[Category:愛知県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:愛知県内の市町村指定史跡]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Burial Mound in Japan |name = Kōyashiki Burial Mound |image = {{Image requested|cat=Komaki City}} |image_caption = |alternate_name = |group = [[Koki Burial Mounds]] |location = 2-474-1 Koki, Komaki City, Aichi Prefecture |coordinates = |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 16 |latitude_sec = 52.4 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 53 |longitude_sec = 45.0 |ISO = |shape = |dimensions = Diameter: 30 m; Height: 5 m |burial_facility = |artifacts = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror, red pigment |accompanying_tombs = |construction_period = Late 3rd century to 4th century |buried_person = |imperial_grave = |historic_sites = Designated as a historic site by Komaki City |tangible_cultural_properties = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror (Designated by Komaki City) |archaeological_site_number = |notable_features = |map = Japan Aichi |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Kōyashiki Burial Mound''' (甲屋敷古墳, ''Kōyashiki Kofun'') is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) from the [[Kofun period]], located in [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[Koki Burial Mounds]].
== Overview == The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to the [[4th century]]. Approximately two-thirds of the mound has been lost<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kōyashiki Burial Mound|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. During soil extraction work in the early [[Shōwa period]], a large quantity of red [[pigment]] and a [[triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror]] were unearthed<ref name=":0" />. A portion of the burial chamber was discovered during a Komaki City historical survey in [[1975]]<ref name=":0" />. On [[March 25]], [[1978]], the triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror was designated as a tangible [[cultural property]] by Komaki City<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Burial Mound (on display at Komaki City History Museum)|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. On [[August 15]], [[1992]] ([[Heisei]] 4), the burial mound was designated as a historic site by Komaki City<ref name=":0" />.
== Artifacts == * Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror: Measuring 20.8 cm in diameter, it is believed to have been manufactured in Japan during the [[Kofun period]]<ref name=":1" />. The artifact is currently preserved and displayed at the [[Komaki City History Museum]]<ref name=":1" />. * Red pigment
== Location == 2-474-1 Koki, [[Komaki City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]
== Access == * A 3-minute walk from the Koki [[bus stop]] served by [[Meitetsu Bus]].
== Notes == {{Notes}} <references />
== Related Topics == * [[Koki Burial Mounds]] – A general term for the burial mounds in this area.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html Kōyashiki Burial Mound] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Burial Mound] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Burial Mounds]
[[Category:Kofun in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Komaki]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Aichi Prefecture]] [[ja:甲屋敷古墳]]

{{Infobox Burial Mound in Japan |name = Kōyashiki Burial Mound |image = {{Image requested|cat=Komaki City}} |image_caption = |alternate_name = |group = [[Koki Burial Mounds]] |location = 2-474-1 Koki, Komaki City, Aichi Prefecture |coordinates = |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 16 |latitude_sec = 52.4 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 53 |longitude_sec = 45.0 |ISO = |shape = |dimensions = Diameter: 30 m; Height: 5 m |burial_facility = |artifacts = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror, red pigment |accompanying_tombs = |construction_period = Late 3rd century to 4th century |buried_person = |imperial_grave = |historic_sites = Designated as a historic site by Komaki City |tangible_cultural_properties = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror (Designated by Komaki City) |archaeological_site_number = |notable_features = |map = Japan Aichi |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Kōyashiki Burial Mound''' (甲屋敷古墳, ''Kōyashiki Kofun'') is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) from the [[Kofun period]], located in [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[Koki Burial Mounds]].
== Overview == The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to the [[4th century]]. Approximately two-thirds of the mound has been lost<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kōyashiki Burial Mound|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. During soil extraction work in the early [[Shōwa period]], a large quantity of red [[pigment]] and a [[triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror]] were unearthed<ref name=":0" />. A portion of the burial chamber was discovered during a Komaki City historical survey in [[1975]]<ref name=":0" />. On [[March 25]], [[1978]], the triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror was designated as a tangible [[cultural property]] by Komaki City<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Burial Mound (on display at Komaki City History Museum)|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. On [[August 15]], [[1992]] ([[Heisei]] 4), the burial mound was designated as a historic site by Komaki City<ref name=":0" />.
== Artifacts == * Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror: Measuring 20.8 cm in diameter, it is believed to have been manufactured in Japan during the [[Kofun period]]<ref name=":1" />. The artifact is currently preserved and displayed at the [[Komaki City History Museum]]<ref name=":1" />. * Red pigment
== Location == 2-474-1 Koki, [[Komaki City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]
== Access == * A 3-minute walk from the Koki [[bus stop]] served by [[Meitetsu Bus]].
== Notes == {{Notes}} <references />
== Related Topics == * [[Koki Burial Mounds]] – A general term for the burial mounds in this area.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html Kōyashiki Burial Mound] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Burial Mound] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Burial Mounds]

'''Kōyashiki Burial Mound''' (甲屋敷古墳, ''Kōyashiki Kofun'') is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) from the [[Kofun period]], located in [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[Koki Burial Mounds]].
== Overview == The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to the [[4th century]]. Approximately two-thirds of the mound has been lost<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kōyashiki Burial Mound|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. During soil extraction work in the early [[Shōwa period]], a large quantity of red [[pigment]] and a [[triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror]] were unearthed<ref name=":0" />. A portion of the burial chamber was discovered during a Komaki City historical survey in [[1975]]<ref name=":0" />. On [[March 25]], [[1978]], the triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror was designated as a tangible [[cultural property]] by Komaki City<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Burial Mound (on display at Komaki City History Museum)|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. On [[August 15]], [[1992]] ([[Heisei]] 4), the burial mound was designated as a historic site by Komaki City<ref name=":0" />.
== Artifacts == * Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror: Measuring 20.8 cm in diameter, it is believed to have been manufactured in Japan during the [[Kofun period]]<ref name=":1" />. The artifact is currently preserved and displayed at the [[Komaki City History Museum]]<ref name=":1" />. * Red pigment
== Location == 2-474-1 Koki, [[Komaki City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]
== Access == * A 3-minute walk from the Koki [[bus stop]] served by [[Meitetsu Bus]].
== Notes == {{Notes}} <references />
== Related Topics == * [[Koki Burial Mounds]] – A general term for the burial mounds in this area.

'''Kōyashiki Burial Mound''' (甲屋敷古墳, ''Kōyashiki Kofun'') is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) from the [[Kofun period]], located in [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[Koki Burial Mounds]].
== Overview == The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to the [[4th century]]. Approximately two-thirds of the mound has been lost<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kōyashiki Burial Mound|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. During soil extraction work in the early [[Shōwa period]], a large quantity of red [[pigment]] and a [[triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror]] were unearthed<ref name=":0" />. A portion of the burial chamber was discovered during a Komaki City historical survey in [[1975]]<ref name=":0" />. On [[March 25]], [[1978]], the triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror was designated as a tangible [[cultural property]] by Komaki City<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Burial Mound (on display at Komaki City History Museum)|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. On [[August 15]], [[1992]] ([[Heisei]] 4), the burial mound was designated as a historic site by Komaki City<ref name=":0" />.
== Artifacts == * Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror: Measuring 20.8 cm in diameter, it is believed to have been manufactured in Japan during the [[Kofun period]]<ref name=":1" />. The artifact is currently preserved and displayed at the [[Komaki City History Museum]]<ref name=":1" />. * Red pigment
== Location == 2-474-1 Koki, [[Komaki City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]
== Access == * A 3-minute walk from the Koki [[bus stop]] served by [[Meitetsu Bus]].
== Notes == {{Notes}} <references />

{{Infobox Burial Mound in Japan |name = Kōyashiki Burial Mound |image = {{Image requested|cat=Komaki City}} |image_caption = |alternate_name = |group = [[Koki Burial Mounds]] |location = 2-474-1 Koki, Komaki City, Aichi Prefecture |coordinates = |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 16 |latitude_sec = 52.4 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 53 |longitude_sec = 45.0 |ISO = |shape = |dimensions = Diameter: 30 m; Height: 5 m |burial_facility = |artifacts = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror, red pigment |accompanying_tombs = |construction_period = Late 3rd century to 4th century |buried_person = |imperial_grave = |historic_sites = Designated as a historic site by Komaki City |tangible_cultural_properties = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror (Designated by Komaki City) |archaeological_site_number = |notable_features = |map = Japan Aichi |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Kōyashiki Burial Mound''' (甲屋敷古墳, ''Kōyashiki Kofun'') is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) from the [[Kofun period]], located in [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[Koki Burial Mounds]].
== Overview == The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to the [[4th century]]. Approximately two-thirds of the mound has been lost<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kōyashiki Burial Mound|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. During soil extraction work in the early [[Shōwa period]], a large quantity of red [[pigment]] and a [[triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror]] were unearthed<ref name=":0" />. A portion of the burial chamber was discovered during a Komaki City historical survey in [[1975]]<ref name=":0" />. On [[March 25]], [[1978]], the triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror was designated as a tangible [[cultural property]] by Komaki City<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Burial Mound (on display at Komaki City History Museum)|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. On [[August 15]], [[1992]] ([[Heisei]] 4), the burial mound was designated as a historic site by Komaki City<ref name=":0" />.
== Artifacts == * Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror: Measuring 20.8 cm in diameter, it is believed to have been manufactured in Japan during the [[Kofun period]]<ref name=":1" />. The artifact is currently preserved and displayed at the [[Komaki City History Museum]]<ref name=":1" />. * Red pigment
== Location == 2-474-1 Koki, [[Komaki City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]
== Access == * A 3-minute walk from the Koki [[bus stop]] served by [[Meitetsu Bus]].
== Notes == {{Notes}} <references />
== See Also == * [[Koki Burial Mounds]] – A general term for the burial mounds in this area.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html Kōyashiki Burial Mound] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Burial Mound] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Burial Mounds]
[[Category:Kofun in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Komaki]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōyashiki Burial Mound]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kōyashiki Burial Mound-qq]] [[Category:Kōyashiki Burial Mound]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kōyashiki Burial Mound-qq]] [[Category:Kōyashiki Burial Mound]][[Category:Kōyashiki Burial Mound]][[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kōyashiki Burial Mound]][[Category:kk]] [[sn:Kōyashiki Burial Mound]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōyashiki Burial Mound]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōyashiki Burial Mound]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:甲屋敷古墳]]

{{Infobox Burial Mound in Japan |name = Kōyashiki Burial Mound |image = {{Image requested|cat=Komaki City}} |image_caption = |alternate_name = |group = [[Koki Burial Mounds]] |location = 2-474-1 Koki, Komaki City, Aichi Prefecture |coordinates = |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 16 |latitude_sec = 52.4 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 53 |longitude_sec = 45.0 |ISO = |shape = |dimensions = Diameter: 30 m; Height: 5 m |burial_facility = |artifacts = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror, red pigment |accompanying_tombs = |construction_period = Late 3rd century to 4th century |buried_person = |imperial_grave = |historic_sites = Designated as a historic site by Komaki City |tangible_cultural_properties = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror (Designated by Komaki City) |archaeological_site_number = |notable_features = |map = Japan Aichi |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Kōyashiki Burial Mound''' (甲屋敷古墳, ''Kōyashiki Kofun'') is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) from the [[Kofun period]], located in [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[Koki Burial Mounds]].
== Overview == The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to the [[4th century]]. Approximately two-thirds of the mound has been lost<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kōyashiki Burial Mound|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. During soil extraction work in the early [[Shōwa period]], a large quantity of red [[pigment]] and a [[triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror]] were unearthed<ref name=":0" />. A portion of the burial chamber was discovered during a Komaki City historical survey in [[1975]]<ref name=":0" />. On [[March 25]], [[1978]], the triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror was designated as a tangible [[cultural property]] by Komaki City<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Burial Mound (on display at Komaki City History Museum)|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. On [[August 15]], [[1992]] ([[Heisei]] 4), the burial mound was designated as a historic site by Komaki City<ref name=":0" />.
== Artifacts == * Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror: Measuring 20.8 cm in diameter, it is believed to have been manufactured in Japan during the [[Kofun period]]<ref name=":1" />. The artifact is currently preserved and displayed at the [[Komaki City History Museum]]<ref name=":1" />. * Red pigment
== Location == 2-474-1 Koki, [[Komaki City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]
== Access == * A 3-minute walk from the Koki [[bus stop]] served by [[Meitetsu Bus]].
== Notes == {{Notes}} <references />
== See Also == * [[Koki Burial Mounds]] – A general term for the burial mounds in this area.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html Kōyashiki Burial Mound] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Burial Mound] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Burial Mounds]

{{Infobox Kofun in Japan |name = Kōyashiki Kofun |image = {{Image requested|cat=Komaki City}} |image_caption = |alternate_name = |group = [[Koki Kofuns]] |location = 2-474-1 Koki, Komaki City, Aichi Prefecture |coordinates = |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 16 |latitude_sec = 52.4 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 53 |longitude_sec = 45.0 |ISO = |shape = |dimensions = Diameter: 30 m; Height: 5 m |burial_facility = |artifacts = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror, red pigment |accompanying_tombs = |construction_period = Late 3rd century to 4th century |buried_person = |imperial_grave = |historic_sites = Designated as a historic site by Komaki City |tangible_cultural_properties = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror (Designated by Komaki City) |archaeological_site_number = |notable_features = |map = Japan Aichi |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Kōyashiki Kofun''' (甲屋敷古墳, ''Kōyashiki Kofun'') is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) from the [[Kofun period]], located in [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[Koki Kofuns]].
== Overview == The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to the [[4th century]]. Approximately two-thirds of the mound has been lost<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kōyashiki Kofun|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. During soil extraction work in the early [[Shōwa period]], a large quantity of red [[pigment]] and a [[triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror]] were unearthed<ref name=":0" />. A portion of the burial chamber was discovered during a Komaki City historical survey in [[1975]]<ref name=":0" />. On [[March 25]], [[1978]], the triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror was designated as a tangible [[cultural property]] by Komaki City<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun (on display at Komaki City History Museum)|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. On [[August 15]], [[1992]] ([[Heisei]] 4), the Kofun was designated as a historic site by Komaki City<ref name=":0" />.
== Artifacts == * Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror: Measuring 20.8 cm in diameter, it is believed to have been manufactured in Japan during the [[Kofun period]]<ref name=":1" />. The artifact is currently preserved and displayed at the [[Komaki City History Museum]]<ref name=":1" />. * Red pigment
== Location == 2-474-1 Koki, [[Komaki City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]
== Access == * A 3-minute walk from the Koki [[bus stop]] served by [[Meitetsu Bus]].
== Notes == {{Notes}} <references />
== See Also == * [[Koki Kofuns]] – A general term for the Kofuns in this area.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Kofun in Japan |name = Kōyashiki Kofun |image = {{Image requested|cat=Komaki City}} |image_caption = |alternate_name = |group = [[Koki Kofuns]] |location = 2-474-1 Koki, Komaki City, Aichi Prefecture |coordinates = |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 16 |latitude_sec = 52.4 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 53 |longitude_sec = 45.0 |ISO = |shape = |dimensions = Diameter: 30 m; Height: 5 m |burial_facility = |artifacts = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror, red pigment |accompanying_tombs = |construction_period = Late 3rd century to 4th century |buried_person = |imperial_grave = |historic_sites = Designated as a historic site by Komaki City |tangible_cultural_properties = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror (Designated by Komaki City) |archaeological_site_number = |notable_features = |map = Japan Aichi |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Kōyashiki Kofun''' (甲屋敷古墳, ''Kōyashiki Kofun'') is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) from the [[Kofun period]], located in [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[Koki Kofuns]].
== Overview == The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to the [[4th century]]. Approximately two-thirds of the mound has been lost<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kōyashiki Kofun|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. During soil extraction work in the early [[Shōwa period]], a large quantity of red [[pigment]] and a [[triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror]] were unearthed<ref name=":0" />. A portion of the burial chamber was discovered during a Komaki City historical survey in [[1975]]<ref name=":0" />. On [[March 25]], [[1978]], the triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror was designated as a tangible [[cultural property]] by Komaki City<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun (on display at Komaki City History Museum)|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. On [[August 15]], [[1992]] ([[Heisei]] 4), the Kofun was designated as a historic site by Komaki City<ref name=":0" />.
== Artifacts == * Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror: Measuring 20.8 cm in diameter, it is believed to have been manufactured in Japan during the [[Kofun period]]<ref name=":1" />. The artifact is currently preserved and displayed at the [[Komaki City History Museum]]<ref name=":1" />. * Red pigment
== Location == 2-474-1 Koki, [[Komaki City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]
== Access == * A 3-minute walk from the Koki [[bus stop]] served by [[Meitetsu Bus]].
== Notes == {{Notes}} <references />
== See Also == * [[Koki Kofuns]] – A general term for the Kofuns in this area.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun in Japan |name = Kōyashiki Kofun |image = {{Image requested|cat=Komaki City}} |image_caption = |alternate_name = |group = [[Koki Kofuns]] |location = 2-474-1 Koki, Komaki City, Aichi Prefecture |coordinates = |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 16 |latitude_sec = 52.4 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 53 |longitude_sec = 45.0 |ISO = |shape = |dimensions = Diameter: 30 m; Height: 5 m |burial_facility = |burial goods = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror, red pigment |accompanying_tombs = |construction_period = Late 3rd century to 4th century |buried_person = |imperial_grave = |historic_sites = Designated as a historic site by Komaki City |tangible_cultural_properties = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror (Designated by Komaki City) |archaeological_site_number = |notable_features = |map = Japan Aichi |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Kōyashiki Kofun''' (甲屋敷古墳, ''Kōyashiki Kofun'') is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) from the [[Kofun period]], located in [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[Koki Kofuns]].
== Overview == The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to the [[4th century]]. Approximately two-thirds of the mound has been lost<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kōyashiki Kofun|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. During soil extraction work in the early [[Shōwa period]], a large quantity of red [[pigment]] and a [[triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror]] were unearthed<ref name=":0" />. A portion of the burial chamber was discovered during a Komaki City historical survey in [[1975]]<ref name=":0" />. On [[March 25]], [[1978]], the triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror was designated as a tangible [[cultural property]] by Komaki City<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun (on display at Komaki City History Museum)|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. On [[August 15]], [[1992]] ([[Heisei]] 4), the Kofun was designated as a historic site by Komaki City<ref name=":0" />.
== Artifacts == * Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror: Measuring 20.8 cm in diameter, it is believed to have been manufactured in Japan during the [[Kofun period]]<ref name=":1" />. The artifact is currently preserved and displayed at the [[Komaki City History Museum]]<ref name=":1" />. * Red pigment
== Location == 2-474-1 Koki, [[Komaki City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]
== Access == * A 3-minute walk from the Koki [[bus stop]] served by [[Meitetsu Bus]].
== Notes == {{Notes}} <references /> == See Also == * [[Koki Kofuns]] – A general term for the Kofuns in this area.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns]

On [[March 25]], [[1978]], the triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror was designated as a tangible [[cultural property]] by Komaki City<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun (on display at Komaki City History Museum)|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. On [[August 15]], [[1992]] ([[Heisei]] 4), the Kofun was designated as a historic site by Komaki City<ref name=":0" />.
== Artifacts == * Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror: Measuring 20.8 cm in diameter, it is believed to have been manufactured in Japan during the [[Kofun period]]<ref name=":1" />. The artifact is currently preserved and displayed at the [[Komaki City History Museum]]<ref name=":1" />. * Red pigment
== Location == 2-474-1 Koki, [[Komaki City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]
== Access == * A 3-minute walk from the Koki [[bus stop]] served by [[Meitetsu Bus]].

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun in Japan |name = Kōyashiki Kofun |image = {{Image requested|cat=Komaki City}} |image_caption = |alternate_name = |group = [[Koki Kofuns]] |location = 2-474-1 Koki, Komaki City, Aichi Prefecture |coordinates = |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 16 |latitude_sec = 52.4 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 53 |longitude_sec = 45.0 |ISO = |shape = |dimensions = Diameter: 30 m; Height: 5 m |Burial Facility = |burial goods = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror, red pigment |accompanying_tombs = |built = Late 3rd century to 4th century |buried_person = |imperial_grave = |historic_sites = Designated as a historic site by Komaki City |tangible_cultural_properties = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror (Designated by Komaki City) |archaeological_site_number = |notable_features = |map = Japan Aichi |map = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Kōyashiki Kofun''' (甲屋敷古墳, ''Kōyashiki Kofun'') is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) from the [[Kofun period]], located in [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[Koki Kofuns]].
== Overview == The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to the [[4th century]]. Approximately two-thirds of the mound has been lost<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kōyashiki Kofun|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. During soil extraction work in the early [[Shōwa period]], a large quantity of red [[pigment]] and a [[triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror]] were unearthed<ref name=":0" />. A portion of the burial chamber was discovered during a Komaki City historical survey in [[1975]]<ref name=":0" />. On [[March 25]], [[1978]], the triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror was designated as a tangible [[cultural property]] by Komaki City<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun (on display at Komaki City History Museum)|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. On [[August 15]], [[1992]] ([[Heisei]] 4), the Kofun was designated as a historic site by Komaki City<ref name=":0" />.
== Artifacts == * Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror: Measuring 20.8 cm in diameter, it is believed to have been manufactured in Japan during the [[Kofun period]]<ref name=":1" />. The artifact is currently preserved and displayed at the [[Komaki City History Museum]]<ref name=":1" />. * Red pigment
== Location == 2-474-1 Koki, [[Komaki City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]
== Access == * A 3-minute walk from the Koki [[bus stop]] served by [[Meitetsu Bus]].
== Notes == {{Notes}} <references /> == See Also == * [[Koki Kofuns]] – A general term for the Kofuns in this area.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun in Japan |name = Kōyashiki Kofun |image = {{Image requested|cat=Komaki City}} |image_caption = |alternate_name = |group = [[Koki Kofuns]] |location = 2-474-1 Koki, Komaki City, Aichi Prefecture |coordinates = |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 16 |latitude_sec = 52.4 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 53 |longitude_sec = 45.0 |ISO = |type = |dimensions = Diameter: 30 m; Height: 5 m |burial = |burial goods = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror, red pigment |accompanying_tombs = |built = Late 3rd century to 4th century |buried_person = |imperial_grave = |historic_sites = Designated as a historic site by Komaki City |tangible_cultural_properties = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror (Designated by Komaki City) |archaeological_site_number = |notable_features = |map = Japan Aichi |map = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Kōyashiki Kofun''' (甲屋敷古墳, ''Kōyashiki Kofun'') is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) from the [[Kofun period]], located in [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[Koki Kofuns]].
== Overview == The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to the [[4th century]]. Approximately two-thirds of the mound has been lost<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kōyashiki Kofun|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. During soil extraction work in the early [[Shōwa period]], a large quantity of red [[pigment]] and a [[triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror]] were unearthed<ref name=":0" />. A portion of the burial chamber was discovered during a Komaki City historical survey in [[1975]]<ref name=":0" />. On [[March 25]], [[1978]], the triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror was designated as a tangible [[cultural property]] by Komaki City<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun (on display at Komaki City History Museum)|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. On [[August 15]], [[1992]] ([[Heisei]] 4), the Kofun was designated as a historic site by Komaki City<ref name=":0" />.
== Artifacts == * Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror: Measuring 20.8 cm in diameter, it is believed to have been manufactured in Japan during the [[Kofun period]]<ref name=":1" />. The artifact is currently preserved and displayed at the [[Komaki City History Museum]]<ref name=":1" />. * Red pigment
== Location == 2-474-1 Koki, [[Komaki City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]
== Access == * A 3-minute walk from the Koki [[bus stop]] served by [[Meitetsu Bus]].
== Notes == {{Notes}} <references /> == See Also == * [[Koki Kofuns]] – A general term for the Kofuns in this area.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns]

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. '''Kōyashiki Kofun''' (甲屋敷古墳, ''Kōyashiki Kofun'') is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) from the [[Kofun period]], located in [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[Koki Kofuns]].
== Overview == The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to the [[4th century]]. Approximately two-thirds of the mound has been lost<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kōyashiki Kofun|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. During soil extraction work in the early [[Shōwa period]], a large quantity of red [[pigment]] and a [[triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror]] were unearthed<ref name=":0" />. A portion of the burial chamber was discovered during a Komaki City historical survey in [[1975]]<ref name=":0" />. On [[March 25]], [[1978]], the triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror was designated as a tangible [[cultural property]] by Komaki City<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun (on display at Komaki City History Museum)|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. On [[August 15]], [[1992]] ([[Heisei]] 4), the Kofun was designated as a historic site by Komaki City<ref name=":0" />.
== Artifacts == * Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror: Measuring 20.8 cm in diameter, it is believed to have been manufactured in Japan during the [[Kofun period]]<ref name=":1" />. The artifact is currently preserved and displayed at the [[Komaki City History Museum]]<ref name=":1" />. * Red pigment
== location == 2-474-1 Koki, [[Komaki City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]
== Access == * A 3-minute walk from the Koki [[bus stop]] served by [[Meitetsu Bus]].
== Notes == {{Notes}} <references /> == See Also == * [[Koki Kofuns]] – A general term for the Kofuns in this area.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun in Japan |name = Kōyashiki Kofun |image = {{Image requested|cat=Komaki City}} |image_caption = |alternate_name = |group = [[Koki Kofuns]] |location = 2-474-1 Koki, Komaki City, Aichi Prefecture |coordinates = |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 16 |latitude_sec = 52.4 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 53 |longitude_sec = 45.0 |omitted = |type = |dimensions = Diameter: 30 m; Height: 5 m |burial = |burial goods = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror, red pigment |accompanying_tombs = |built = Late 3rd century to 4th century |buried_person = |imperial_grave = |historic_sites = Designated as a historic site by Komaki City |tangible_cultural_properties = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror (Designated by Komaki City) |archaeological_site_number = |notable_features = |map = Japan Aichi |map = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Kōyashiki Kofun''' (甲屋敷古墳, ''Kōyashiki Kofun'') is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) from the [[Kofun period]], located in [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[Koki Kofuns]].
== Overview == The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to the [[4th century]]. Approximately two-thirds of the mound has been lost<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kōyashiki Kofun|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. During soil extraction work in the early [[Shōwa period]], a large quantity of red [[pigment]] and a [[triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror]] were unearthed<ref name=":0" />. A portion of the burial chamber was discovered during a Komaki City historical survey in [[1975]]<ref name=":0" />. On [[March 25]], [[1978]], the triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror was designated as a tangible [[cultural property]] by Komaki City<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun (on display at Komaki City History Museum)|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. On [[August 15]], [[1992]] ([[Heisei]] 4), the Kofun was designated as a historic site by Komaki City<ref name=":0" />.
== Artifacts == * Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror: Measuring 20.8 cm in diameter, it is believed to have been manufactured in Japan during the [[Kofun period]]<ref name=":1" />. The artifact is currently preserved and displayed at the [[Komaki City History Museum]]<ref name=":1" />. * Red pigment
== location == 2-474-1 Koki, [[Komaki City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]
== Access == * A 3-minute walk from the Koki [[bus stop]] served by [[Meitetsu Bus]].
== Notes == {{Notes}} <references /> == See Also == * [[Koki Kofuns]] – A general term for the Kofuns in this area.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns]

'''Kōyashiki Kofun''' (甲屋敷古墳, ''Kōyashiki Kofun'') is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) from the [[Kofun period]], located in [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[Koki Kofuns]].
== Overview == The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to the [[4th century]]. Approximately two-thirds of the mound has been lost<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kōyashiki Kofun|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. During soil extraction work in the early [[Shōwa period]], a large quantity of red [[pigment]] and a [[triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror]] were unearthed<ref name=":0" />. A portion of the burial chamber was discovered during a Komaki City historical survey in [[1975]]<ref name=":0" />. On [[March 25]], [[1978]], the triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror was designation1 as a tangible [[cultural property]] by Komaki City<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun (on display at Komaki City History Museum)|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. On [[August 15]], [[1992]] ([[Heisei]] 4), the Kofun was designation1 as a historic site by Komaki City<ref name=":0" />.
== burial goods == * Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror: Measuring 20.8 cm in diameter, it is believed to have been manufactured in Japan during the [[Kofun period]]<ref name=":1" />. The artifact is currently preserved and displayed at the [[Komaki City History Museum]]<ref name=":1" />. * Red pigment
== location == 2-474-1 Koki, [[Komaki City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]
== Access == * A 3-minute walk from the Koki [[bus stop]] served by [[Meitetsu Bus]].
== notes == {{notes}} <references /> == See Also == * [[Koki Kofuns]] – A general term for the Kofuns in this area.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns]

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. '''Kōyashiki Kofun''' (甲屋敷古墳, ''Kōyashiki Kofun'') is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) from the [[Kofun period]], located in [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[Koki Kofuns]].
== Overview == The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to the [[4th century]]. Approximately two-thirds of the mound has been lost<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kōyashiki Kofun|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. During soil extraction work in the early [[Shōwa period]], a large quantity of red [[pigment]] and a [[triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror]] were unearthed<ref name=":0" />. A portion of the burial chamber was discovered during a Komaki City historical survey in [[1975]]<ref name=":0" />. On [[March 25]], [[1978]], the triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror was other_designation1 as a tangible [[cultural property]] by Komaki City<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun (on display at Komaki City History Museum)|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. On [[August 15]], [[1992]] ([[Heisei]] 4), the Kofun was other_designation1 as a historic site by Komaki City<ref name=":0" />.
== burial goods == * Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror: Measuring 20.8 cm in diameter, it is believed to have been manufactured in Japan during the [[Kofun period]]<ref name=":1" />. The artifact is currently preserved and displayed at the [[Komaki City History Museum]]<ref name=":1" />. * Red pigment
== location == 2-474-1 Koki, [[Komaki City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]
== Access == * A 3-minute walk from the Koki [[bus stop]] served by [[Meitetsu Bus]].
== notes == {{notes}} <references /> == See Also == * [[Koki Kofuns]] – A general term for the Kofuns in this area.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun in Japan |name = Kōyashiki Kofun |image = {{Image requested|cat=Komaki City}} |image_caption = |alternate_name = |group = [[Koki Kofuns]] |location = 2-474-1 Koki, Komaki City, Aichi Prefecture |coordinates = |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 16 |coordinates = 52.4 |coordinates = 136 |coordinates = 53 |coordinates = 45.0 |omitted = |type = |area = Diameter: 30 m; Height: 5 m |burial = |burial goods = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror, red pigment |accompanying_tombs = |built = Late 3rd century to 4th century |buried_person = |imperial_grave = |historic_sites = Designated as a historic site by Komaki City |tangible_cultural_properties = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror (Designated by Komaki City) |archaeological_site_number = |notable_features = |map = Japan Aichi |map = |type = |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = }}
'''Kōyashiki Kofun''' (甲屋敷古墳, ''Kōyashiki Kofun'') is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) from the [[Kofun period]], located in [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[Koki Kofuns]].
== Overview == The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to the [[4th century]]. Approximately two-thirds of the mound has been lost<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kōyashiki Kofun|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. During soil extraction work in the early [[Shōwa period]], a large quantity of red [[pigment]] and a [[triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror]] were unearthed<ref name=":0" />. A portion of the burial chamber was discovered during a Komaki City historical survey in [[1975]]<ref name=":0" />. On [[March 25]], [[1978]], the triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror was other_designation1 as a tangible [[cultural property]] by Komaki City<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun (on display at Komaki City History Museum)|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. On [[August 15]], [[1992]] ([[Heisei]] 4), the Kofun was other_designation1 as a historic site by Komaki City<ref name=":0" />.
== burial goods == * Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror: Measuring 20.8 cm in diameter, it is believed to have been manufactured in Japan during the [[Kofun period]]<ref name=":1" />. The artifact is currently preserved and displayed at the [[Komaki City History Museum]]<ref name=":1" />. * Red pigment
== location == 2-474-1 Koki, [[Komaki City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]
== Access == * A 3-minute walk from the Koki [[bus stop]] served by [[Meitetsu Bus]].
== notes == {{notes}} <references /> == See Also == * [[Koki Kofuns]] – A general term for the Kofuns in this area.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns]

== burial goods == * Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror: Measuring 20.8 cm in diameter, it is believed to have been manufactured in Japan during the [[Kofun period]]<ref name=":1" />. The artifact is currently preserved and displayed at the [[Komaki City History Museum]]<ref name=":1" />. * Red pigment
== location == 2-474-1 Koki, [[Komaki City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]
== Access == * A 3-minute walk from the Koki [[bus stop]] served by [[Meitetsu Bus]].

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun in Japan |name = Kōyashiki Kofun |image = {{Image requested|cat=Komaki City}} |image_caption = |alternate_name = |group = [[Koki Kofuns]] |location = 2-474-1 Koki, Komaki City, Aichi Prefecture |coordinates = |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 16 |coordinates = 52.4 |coordinates = 136 |coordinates = 53 |coordinates = 45.0 |omitted = |type = |area = Diameter: 30 m; Height: 5 m |burial = |burial goods = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror, red pigment |accompanying_tombs = |built = Late 3rd century to 4th century |buried_person = |imperial_grave = |historic_sites = Designated as a historic site by Komaki City |tangible_cultural_properties = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror (Designated by Komaki City) |archaeological_site_number = |notable_features = |map = Japan Aichi |map = |type = |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = }}
'''Kōyashiki Kofun''' (甲屋敷古墳, ''Kōyashiki Kofun'') is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) from the [[Kofun period]], located in [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[Koki Kofuns]].
== Overview == The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to the [[4th century]]. Approximately two-thirds of the mound has been lost<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kōyashiki Kofun|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. During soil extraction work in the early [[Shōwa period]], a large quantity of red [[pigment]] and a [[triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror]] were unearthed<ref name=":0" />. A portion of the burial chamber was discovered during a Komaki City historical survey in [[1975]]<ref name=":0" />. On [[March 25]], [[1978]], the triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror was other_designation1 as a tangible [[cultural property]] by Komaki City<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun (on display at Komaki City History Museum)|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. On [[August 15]], [[1992]] ([[Heisei]] 4), the Kofun was other_designation1 as a historic site by Komaki City<ref name=":0" />.
== burial goods == * Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror: Measuring 20.8 cm in diameter, it is believed to have been manufactured in Japan during the [[Kofun period]]<ref name=":1" />. The artifact is currently preserved and displayed at the [[Komaki City History Museum]]<ref name=":1" />. * Red pigment
== location == 2-474-1 Koki, [[Komaki City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]
== Access == * A 3-minute walk from the Koki [[bus stop]] served by [[Meitetsu Bus]].
== notes == {{notes}} <references /> == See Also == * [[Koki Kofuns]] – A general term for the Kofuns in this area.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns]

'''Kōyashiki Kofun''' (甲屋敷古墳, ''Kōyashiki Kofun'') is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) from the [[Kofun period]], located in [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[Koki Kofuns]].
== Overview == The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to the [[4th century]]. Approximately two-thirds of the mound has been lost<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kōyashiki Kofun|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. During soil extraction work in the early [[Shōwa period]], a large quantity of red [[pigment]] and a [[triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror]] were unearthed<ref name=":0" />. A portion of the burial chamber was discovered during a Komaki City historical survey in [[1975]]<ref name=":0" />. On [[March 25]], [[1978]], the triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror was other_designation1 as a tangible [[cultural property]] by Komaki City<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun (on display at Komaki City History Museum)|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. On [[August 15]], [[1992]] ([[Heisei]] 4), the Kofun was other_designation1 as a historic site by Komaki City<ref name=":0" />.
== burial goods == * Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror: Measuring 20.8 cm in diameter, it is believed to have been manufactured in Japan during the [[Kofun period]]<ref name=":1" />. The artifact is currently preserved and displayed at the [[Komaki City History Museum]]<ref name=":1" />. * Red pigment
== location == 2-474-1 Koki, [[Komaki City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]
== Access == * A 3-minute walk from the Koki [[bus stop]] served by [[Meitetsu Bus]].
== notes == {{notes}} <references /> == See Also == * [[Koki Kofuns]] – A general term for the Kofuns in this area.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns]
