This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun in Japan |name = Kōyashiki Kofun |image = {{Image requested|cat=Komaki City}} |image_caption = |alternate_name = |group = [[Koki Kofuns]] |location = 2-474-1 Koki, Komaki City, Aichi Prefecture |coordinates = |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 16 |coordinates = 52.4 |coordinates = 136 |coordinates = 53 |coordinates = 45.0 |omitted = |type = |area = Diameter: 30 m; Height: 5 m |burial = |burial goods = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror, red pigment |accompanying_tombs = |built = Late 3rd century to 4th century |buried_person = |imperial_grave = |historic_sites = Designated as a historic site by Komaki City |tangible_cultural_properties = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror (Designated by Komaki City) |archaeological_site_number = |notable_features = |map = Japan Aichi |map = |type = |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = }}
'''Kōyashiki Kofun''' (甲屋敷古墳, ''Kōyashiki Kofun'') is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) from the [[Kofun period]], located in [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[Koki Kofuns]].
== Overview == The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to the [[4th century]]. Approximately two-thirds of the mound has been lost<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kōyashiki Kofun|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. During soil extraction work in the early [[Shōwa period]], a large quantity of red [[pigment]] and a [[triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror]] were unearthed<ref name=":0" />. A portion of the burial chamber was discovered during a Komaki City historical survey in [[1975]]<ref name=":0" />. On [[March 25]], [[1978]], the triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror was Historic Site1 as a tangible [[cultural property]] by Komaki City<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun (on display at Komaki City History Museum)|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. On [[August 15]], [[1992]] ([[Heisei]] 4), the Kofun was Historic Site1 as a historic site by Komaki City<ref name=":0" />.
== burial goods == * Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror: Measuring 20.8 cm in diameter, it is believed to have been manufactured in Japan during the [[Kofun period]]<ref name=":1" />. The artifact is currently preserved and displayed at the [[Komaki City History Museum]]<ref name=":1" />. * Red pigment
== location == 2-474-1 Koki, [[Komaki City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]
== Access == * A 3-minute walk from the Koki [[bus stop]] served by [[Meitetsu Bus]].
== notes == {{notes}} <references /> == See Also == * [[Koki Kofuns]] – A general term for the Kofuns in this area.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun in Japan |name = Kōyashiki Kofun |image = {{Image requested|cat=Komaki City}} |image_caption = |alternate_name = |group = [[Koki Kofuns]] |location = 2-474-1 Koki, Komaki City, Aichi Prefecture |coordinates = |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 16 |coordinates = 52.4 |coordinates = 136 |coordinates = 53 |coordinates = 45.0 |omitted = |type = |area = Diameter: 30 m; Height: 5 m |burial = |burial goods = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror, red pigment |accompanying_tombs = |built = Late 3rd century to 4th century |buried_person = |imperial_grave = |historic_sites = Designated as a historic site by Komaki City |tangible_cultural_properties = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror (Designated by Komaki City) |archaeological_site_number = |notable_features = |map = Japan Aichi |map = |type = |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = }}
'''Kōyashiki Kofun''' (甲屋敷古墳, ''Kōyashiki Kofun'') is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) from the [[Kofun period]], located in [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[Koki Kofuns]].
== Overview == The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to the [[4th century]]. Approximately two-thirds of the mound has been lost<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kōyashiki Kofun|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. During soil extraction work in the early [[Shōwa period]], a large quantity of red [[pigment]] and a [[triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror]] were unearthed<ref name=":0" />. A portion of the burial chamber was discovered during a Komaki City historical survey in [[1975]]<ref name=":0" />. On [[March 25]], [[1978]], the triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror was Historic Site1 as a tangible [[cultural property]] by Komaki City<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun (on display at Komaki City History Museum)|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. On [[August 15]], [[1992]] ([[Heisei]] 4), the Kofun was Historic Site1 as a historic site by Komaki City<ref name=":0" />.
== burial goods == * Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror: Measuring 20.8 cm in diameter, it is believed to have been manufactured in Japan during the [[Kofun period]]<ref name=":1" />. The artifact is currently preserved and displayed at the [[Komaki City History Museum]]<ref name=":1" />. * Red pigment
== location == 2-474-1 Koki, [[Komaki City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]
== Access == * A 3-minute walk from the Koki [[bus stop]] served by [[Meitetsu Bus]].
== notes == {{notes}} <references />
== See Also == * [[Koki Kofuns]] – A general term for the Kofuns in this area.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns]
<!--Kōyashiki Kofun-->

The artifact is currently preserved and displayed at the [[Komaki City History Museum]]<ref name=":1" />. * Red pigment
== location == 2-474-1 Koki, [[Komaki City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]
== Access == * A 3-minute walk from the Koki [[bus stop]] served by [[Meitetsu Bus]].
== notes == {{notes}} <references />
== See Also == * [[Koki Kofuns]] – A general term for the Kofuns in this area.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns] <!--Kōyashiki Kofun-->

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun in Japan |name = Kōyashiki Kofun |image = {{Image requested|cat=Komaki City}} |image_caption = |alternate_name = |group = [[Koki Kofuns]] |location = 2-474-1 Koki, Komaki City, Aichi Prefecture |coordinates = |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 16 |coordinates = 52.4 |coordinates = 136 |coordinates = 53 |coordinates = 45.0 |omitted = |type = |area = Diameter: 30 m; Height: 5 m |burial = |burial goods = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror, red pigment |accompanying_tombs = |built = Late 3rd century to 4th century |buried_person = |imperial_grave = |historic_sites = Designated as a historic site by Komaki City |tangible_cultural_properties = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror (Designated by Komaki City) |archaeological_site_number = |notable_features = |map = Japan Aichi |map = |type = |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = }}
'''Kōyashiki Kofun''' (甲屋敷古墳, ''Kōyashiki Kofun'') is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) from the [[Kofun period]], located in [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[Koki Kofuns]].
== Overview == The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to the [[4th century]]. Approximately two-thirds of the mound has been lost<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kōyashiki Kofun|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. During soil extraction work in the early [[Shōwa period]], a large quantity of red [[pigment]] and a [[triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror]] were unearthed<ref name=":0" />. A portion of the burial chamber was discovered during a Komaki City historical survey in [[1975]]<ref name=":0" />. On [[March 25]], [[1978]], the triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror was Historic Site1 as a tangible [[cultural property]] by Komaki City<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun (on display at Komaki City History Museum)|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. On [[August 15]], [[1992]] ([[Heisei]] 4), the Kofun was Historic Site1 as a historic site by Komaki City<ref name=":0" />.
== burial goods == * Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror: Measuring 20.8 cm in diameter, it is believed to have been manufactured in Japan during the [[Kofun period]]<ref name=":1" />. The artifact is currently preserved and displayed at the [[Komaki City History Museum]]<ref name=":1" />. * Red pigment
== location == 2-474-1 Koki, [[Komaki City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]
== Access == * A 3-minute walk from the Koki [[bus stop]] served by [[Meitetsu Bus]].
== notes == {{notes}} <references />
== See Also == * [[Koki Kofuns]] – A general term for the Kofuns in this area.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns] <!--Kōyashiki Kofun-->
<!--Kōyashiki Kofun-->

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 甲屋敷古墳 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=小牧市}} |画像キャプション = |別名 = |所属 = [[小木古墳群]] |所在地 = 愛知県小牧市小木二丁目474番1 |位置 = |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 16|緯度秒 = 52.4 |経度度 = 136|経度分 = 53|経度秒 = 45.0 |ISO = |形状 = |規模 = 直径30m、高さ5m |埋葬施設 = |出土品 = 三角縁神獣鏡、赤の顔料 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = 3世紀末-4世紀 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 小牧市指定史跡 |有形文化財 = 三角縁神獣鏡（小牧市指定） |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Aichi |地図2 = |アイコン = |ラベル = |ラベル位置 = }}
'''甲屋敷古墳'''（こうやしきこふん）とは、[[愛知県]][[小牧市]]にある[[古墳時代]]の[[古墳]]である。[[小木古墳群]]の1つ。
== 概要 == [[3世紀]]末～[[4世紀]]頃に築造されたと推定される。古墳の3分の2はすでに失われてる<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=甲屋敷古墳{{!}}小牧市|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html|website=www.city.komaki.aichi.jp|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>。[[昭和]]初期の土取作業宙に、古墳内から多量の赤い[[顔料]]と[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が出土した<ref name=":0" />。[[1975年]]（昭和50年）の小牧市市史編纂調査により、埋葬主体の一部が発見された<ref name=":0" />。[[1978年]]（昭和53年）[[3月25日]]、三角縁神獣鏡が小牧市の有形[[文化財]]に指定される<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|和書|title=甲屋敷古墳出土の三角縁神獣鏡（小牧市歴史館に展示中）{{!}}小牧市|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html|website=www.city.komaki.aichi.jp|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>。[[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）[[8月15日]]、古墳が小牧市の[[史跡]]に指定された<ref name=":0" />。
== 出土品 == *三角縁神獣鏡 - 直径は20.8cmで、古墳時代に[[日本]]国内で製造されたと考えられている<ref name=":1" />。現在、[[小牧市歴史館]]に保存・展示されている<ref name=":1" />。 *赤い顔料
== 所在地 == 愛知県小牧市小木2丁目474番地1号
== 交通手段 == *[[名鉄バス]]の小木[[バス停留所|停留所]]下車、徒歩3分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references />
== 関連項目 == *[[小木古墳群]] - この古墳を含むこの辺り一帯にある古墳の総称。
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html 甲屋敷古墳] *[http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html 甲屋敷古墳出土の三角縁神獣鏡] *[http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html 小木古墳群]
{{DEFAULTSORT:こうやしきこふん}} [[Category:愛知県の古墳]] [[Category:小牧市の歴史]] [[Category:愛知県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:愛知県内の市町村指定史跡]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 甲屋敷古墳 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=小牧市}} |画像キャプション = |別名 = |所属 = [[小木古墳群]] |所在地 = 愛知県小牧市小木二丁目474番1 |位置 = |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 16|緯度秒 = 52.4 |経度度 = 136|経度分 = 53|経度秒 = 45.0 |ISO = |形状 = |規模 = 直径30m、高さ5m |埋葬施設 = |出土品 = 三角縁神獣鏡、赤の顔料 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = 3世紀末-4世紀 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 小牧市指定史跡 |有形文化財 = 三角縁神獣鏡（小牧市指定） |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Aichi |地図2 = |アイコン = |ラベル = |ラベル位置 = }}
'''甲屋敷古墳'''（こうやしきこふん）とは、[[愛知県]][[小牧市]]にある[[古墳時代]]の[[古墳]]である。[[小木古墳群]]の1つ。
== 概要 == [[3世紀]]末～[[4世紀]]頃に築造されたと推定される。古墳の3分の2はすでに失われてる<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=甲屋敷古墳{{!}}小牧市|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html|website=www.city.komaki.aichi.jp|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>。[[昭和]]初期の土取作業宙に、古墳内から多量の赤い[[顔料]]と[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が出土した<ref name=":0" />。[[1975年]]（昭和50年）の小牧市市史編纂調査により、埋葬主体の一部が発見された<ref name=":0" />。[[1978年]]（昭和53年）[[3月25日]]、三角縁神獣鏡が小牧市の有形[[文化財]]に指定される<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|和書|title=甲屋敷古墳出土の三角縁神獣鏡（小牧市歴史館に展示中）{{!}}小牧市|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html|website=www.city.komaki.aichi.jp|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>。[[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）[[8月15日]]、古墳が小牧市の[[史跡]]に指定された<ref name=":0" />。
== 出土品 == *三角縁神獣鏡 - 直径は20.8cmで、古墳時代に[[日本]]国内で製造されたと考えられている<ref name=":1" />。現在、[[小牧市歴史館]]に保存・展示されている<ref name=":1" />。 *赤い顔料
== 所在地 == 愛知県小牧市小木2丁目474番地1号
== 交通手段 == *[[名鉄バス]]の小木[[バス停留所|停留所]]下車、徒歩3分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references />
== 関連項目 == *[[小木古墳群]] - この古墳を含むこの辺り一帯にある古墳の総称。
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html 甲屋敷古墳] *[http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html 甲屋敷古墳出土の三角縁神獣鏡] *[http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html 小木古墳群]
{{DEFAULTSORT:こうやしきこふん}} [[Category:愛知県の古墳]] [[Category:小牧市の歴史]] [[Category:愛知県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:愛知県内の市町村指定史跡]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Kōyashiki Kofun |image = {{Image requested|cat=Komaki City}} |image_caption = |alternate_name = |group = [[Koki Kofun Cluster]] |location = {{ill|Komaki|qid=Q389537}} |coordinates = |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 16 |coordinates = 52.4 |coordinates = 136 |coordinates = 53 |coordinates = 45.0 |omitted = |type = |area = Diameter: 30 m; Height: 5 m |burial = |burial goods = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror, red pigment |accompanying_tombs = |built = Late 3rd century to 4th century |buried_person = |imperial_grave = |historic_sites = Designated as a historic site by Komaki City |tangible_cultural_properties = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror (Designated by Komaki City) |archaeological_site_number = |notable_features = |map = Japan Aichi |map = |type = |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = }}
'''Kōyashiki Kofun''' (甲屋敷古墳, ''Kōyashiki Kofun'') is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) from the [[Kofun period]], located in [[:en:Komaki|Komaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[Koki Kofun Cluster]].
== Overview == The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to the [[:en:4th century|4th century]]. Approximately two-thirds of the mound has been lost<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kōyashiki Kofun|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. During soil extraction work in the early [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]], a large quantity of red [[:en:Pigment|Pigment]] and a [[Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror]] were unearthed<ref name=":0" />. A portion of the burial chamber was discovered during a Komaki City historical survey in [[:en:1975|1975]]<ref name=":0" />. On [[:en:March 25|March 25]], [[:en:1978|1978]], the triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror was Historic Site1 as a tangible [[:en:Cultural property|Cultural property]] by Komaki City<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun (on display at Komaki City History Museum)|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. On [[:en:August 15|August 15]], [[:en:1992|1992]] ([[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 4), the Kofun was Historic Site1 as a historic site by Komaki City<ref name=":0" />.
== burial goods == * Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror: Measuring 20.8 cm in diameter, it is believed to have been manufactured in Japan during the [[Kofun period]]<ref name=":1" />. The artifact is currently preserved and displayed at the [[Komaki City History Museum]]<ref name=":1" />. * Red pigment
== location == 2-474-1 Koki, [[Komaki City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]
== Access == * A 3-minute walk from the Koki [[:en:Bus stop|Bus stop]] served by [[:en:Meitetsu Bus|Meitetsu Bus]].
== notes == {{notes}} <references />
== See Also == * [[Koki Kofun Cluster]] – A general term for the Kofuns in this area.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns]
{{translated page|ja|甲屋敷古墳|version=98203992|comment=Imported full ja history}}

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kōyashiki Kofun |image = {{Image requested|cat=Komaki City}} |image_caption = |alternate_name = |group = [[Koki Kofun Cluster]] |location = {{ill|Komaki|WD=Q389537}} |coordinates = |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 16 |coordinates = 52.4 |coordinates = 136 |coordinates = 53 |coordinates = 45.0 |omitted = |type = |area = Diameter: 30 m; Height: 5 m |burial = |burial goods = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror, red pigment |accompanying_tombs = |built = Late 3rd century to 4th century |buried_person = |imperial_grave = |historic_sites = Designated as a historic site by Komaki City |tangible_cultural_properties = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror (Designated by Komaki City) |archaeological_site_number = |notable_features = |map = Japan Aichi |map = |type = |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = }}
'''Kōyashiki Kofun''' (甲屋敷古墳, ''Kōyashiki Kofun'') is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) from the [[Kofun period]], located in [[:en:Komaki|Komaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[Koki Kofun Cluster]].
== Overview == The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to the [[:en:4th century|4th century]]. Approximately two-thirds of the mound has been lost<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kōyashiki Kofun|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. During soil extraction work in the early [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]], a large quantity of red [[:en:Pigment|Pigment]] and a [[Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror]] were unearthed<ref name=":0" />. A portion of the burial chamber was discovered during a Komaki City historical survey in [[:en:1975|1975]]<ref name=":0" />. On [[:en:March 25|March 25]], [[:en:1978|1978]], the triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror was Historic Site1 as a tangible [[:en:Cultural property|Cultural property]] by Komaki City<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun (on display at Komaki City History Museum)|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. On [[:en:August 15|August 15]], [[:en:1992|1992]] ([[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 4), the Kofun was Historic Site1 as a historic site by Komaki City<ref name=":0" />.
== burial goods == * Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror: Measuring 20.8 cm in diameter, it is believed to have been manufactured in Japan during the [[Kofun period]]<ref name=":1" />. The artifact is currently preserved and displayed at the [[Komaki City History Museum]]<ref name=":1" />. * Red pigment
== location == 2-474-1 Koki, [[Komaki City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]
== Access == * A 3-minute walk from the Koki [[:en:Bus stop|Bus stop]] served by [[:en:Meitetsu Bus|Meitetsu Bus]].
== notes == {{notes}} <references />
== See Also == * [[Koki Kofun Cluster]] – A general term for the Kofuns in this area.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Kōyashiki Kofun |image = {{Image requested|cat=Komaki City}} |image_caption = |alternate_name = |group = [[Koki Kofun Cluster]] |location = {{ill|Komaki|WD=Q389537}} |coordinates = |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 16 |coordinates = 52.4 |coordinates = 136 |coordinates = 53 |coordinates = 45.0 |omitted = |type = |area = Diameter: 30 m; Height: 5 m |burial = |burial goods = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror, red pigment |accompanying_tombs = |built = Late 3rd century to 4th century |buried_person = |imperial_grave = |historic_sites = Designated as a historic site by Komaki City |tangible_cultural_properties = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror (Designated by Komaki City) |archaeological_site_number = |notable_features = |map = Japan Aichi |map = |type = |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = }}
'''Kōyashiki Kofun''' (甲屋敷古墳, ''Kōyashiki Kofun'') is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) from the [[Kofun period]], located in [[:en:Komaki|Komaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[Koki Kofun Cluster]].
== Overview == The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to the [[:en:4th century|4th century]]. Approximately two-thirds of the mound has been lost<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kōyashiki Kofun|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. During soil extraction work in the early [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]], a large quantity of red [[:en:Pigment|Pigment]] and a [[Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror]] were unearthed<ref name=":0" />. A portion of the burial chamber was discovered during a Komaki City historical survey in [[:en:1975|1975]]<ref name=":0" />. On [[:en:March 25|March 25]], [[:en:1978|1978]], the triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror was Historic Site1 as a tangible [[:en:Cultural property|Cultural property]] by Komaki City<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun (on display at Komaki City History Museum)|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. On [[:en:August 15|August 15]], [[:en:1992|1992]] ([[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 4), the Kofun was Historic Site1 as a historic site by Komaki City<ref name=":0" />.
== burial goods == * Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror: Measuring 20.8 cm in diameter, it is believed to have been manufactured in Japan during the [[Kofun period]]<ref name=":1" />. The artifact is currently preserved and displayed at the [[Komaki City History Museum]]<ref name=":1" />. * Red pigment
== location == 2-474-1 Koki, [[Komaki City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]
== Access == * A 3-minute walk from the Koki [[:en:Bus stop|Bus stop]] served by [[:en:Meitetsu Bus|Meitetsu Bus]].
== notes == {{notes}} <references />
== See Also == * [[Koki Kofun Cluster]] – A general term for the Kofuns in this area.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Kōyashiki Kofun |image = {{Image requested|cat=Komaki City}} |image_caption = |alternate_name = |group = [[Koki Kofun Cluster]] |location = {{ill|Komaki|WD=Q389537}} |coordinates = |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 16 |coordinates = 52.4 |coordinates = 136 |coordinates = 53 |coordinates = 45.0 |omitted = |type = |area = Diameter: 30 m; Height: 5 m |burial = |burial goods = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror, red pigment |accompanying_tombs = |built = Late 3rd century to 4th century |buried_person = |imperial_grave = |historic_sites = Designated as a historic site by Komaki City |tangible_cultural_properties = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror (Designated by Komaki City) |archaeological_site_number = |notable_features = |map = Japan Aichi |map = |type = |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = }}
'''Kōyashiki Kofun''' (甲屋敷古墳, ''Kōyashiki Kofun'') is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) from the [[Kofun period]], located in [[:en:Komaki|Komaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[Koki Kofun Cluster]].
== Overview == The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to the [[:en:4th century|4th century]]. Approximately two-thirds of the mound has been lost<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kōyashiki Kofun|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. During soil extraction work in the early [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]], a large quantity of red [[:en:Pigment|Pigment]] and a [[Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror]] were unearthed<ref name=":0" />. A portion of the burial chamber was discovered during a Komaki City historical survey in [[:en:1975|1975]]<ref name=":0" />. On [[:en:March 25|March 25]], [[:en:1978|1978]], the triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror was Historic Site1 as a tangible [[:en:Cultural property|Cultural property]] by Komaki City<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun (on display at Komaki City History Museum)|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. On [[:en:August 15|August 15]], [[:en:1992|1992]] ([[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 4), the Kofun was Historic Site1 as a historic site by Komaki City<ref name=":0" />.
== burial goods == * Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror: Measuring 20.8 cm in diameter, it is believed to have been manufactured in Japan during the [[Kofun period]]<ref name=":1" />. The artifact is currently preserved and displayed at the [[Komaki City History Museum]]<ref name=":1" />. * Red pigment
== location == 2-474-1 Koki, [[Komaki City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]
== Access == * A 3-minute walk from the Koki [[:en:Bus stop|Bus stop]] served by [[:en:Meitetsu Bus|Meitetsu Bus]].
== notes == {{notes}} <references />
== See Also == * [[Koki Kofun Cluster]] – A general term for the Kofuns in this area.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns]

On [[:en:August 15|August 15]], [[:en:1992|1992]] ([[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 4), the Kofun was Historic Site1 as a historic site by Komaki City<ref name=":0" />.

'''Kōyashiki Kofun''' (甲屋敷古墳, ''Kōyashiki Kofun'') is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) from the [[Kofun period]], located in [[:en:Komaki|Komaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[Koki Kofun Cluster]].
== Overview == The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to the [[:en:4th century|4th century]]. Approximately two-thirds of the mound has been lost<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kōyashiki Kofun|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. During soil extraction work in the early [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]], a large quantity of red [[:en:Pigment|Pigment]] and a [[Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror]] were unearthed<ref name=":0" />. A portion of the burial chamber was discovered during a Komaki City historical survey in [[:en:1975|1975]]<ref name=":0" />. On [[:en:March 25|March 25]], [[:en:1978|1978]], the triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror was Historic Site1 as a tangible [[:en:Cultural property|Cultural property]] by Komaki City<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun (on display at Komaki City History Museum)|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. On [[:en:August 15|August 15]], [[:en:1992|1992]] ([[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 4), the Kofun was Historic Site1 as a historic site by Komaki City<ref name=":0" />.
== burial goods == * Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror: Measuring 20.8 cm in diameter, it is believed to have been manufactured in Japan during the [[Kofun period]]<ref name=":1" />. The artifact is currently preserved and displayed at the [[Komaki City History Museum]]<ref name=":1" />. * Red pigment
== location == 2-474-1 Koki, [[Komaki City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]
== Access == * A 3-minute walk from the Koki [[:en:Bus stop|Bus stop]] served by [[:en:Meitetsu Bus|Meitetsu Bus]].
== notes == {{notes}} <references />
== See Also == * [[Koki Kofun Cluster]] – A general term for the Kofuns in this area.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns]

Approximately two-thirds of the mound has been lost<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kōyashiki Kofun|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. During soil extraction work in the early {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era}}, a large quantity of red {{ill|Pigment|en|Pigment|WD=Q161179|lt=Pigment}} and a [[Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror]] were unearthed<ref name=":0" />. A portion of the burial chamber was discovered during a Komaki City historical survey in {{ill|1975|en|1975|WD=Q2479|lt=1975}}<ref name=":0" />. On {{ill|March 25|en|March 25|WD=Q2454|lt=March 25}}, {{ill|1978|en|1978|WD=Q2483|lt=1978}}, the triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror was Historic Site1 as a tangible {{ill|Cultural property|en|Cultural property|WD=Q2065736|lt=Cultural property}} by Komaki City<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun (on display at Komaki City History Museum)|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. On {{ill|August 15|en|August 15|WD=Q2774|lt=August 15}}, {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992}} ([[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 4), the Kofun was Historic Site1 as a historic site by Komaki City<ref name=":0" />.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:甲屋敷古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:甲屋敷古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:甲屋敷古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Kōyashiki Kofun |image = {{Image requested|cat=Komaki City}} |image_caption = |alternate_name = |group = [[Koki Kofun Cluster]] |location = {{ill|Komaki|WD=Q389537|1=Komaki}} |coordinates = |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 16 |coordinates = 52.4 |coordinates = 136 |coordinates = 53 |coordinates = 45.0 |omitted = |type = |area = Diameter: 30 m; Height: 5 m |burial = |burial goods = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror, red pigment |accompanying_tombs = |built = Late 3rd century to 4th century |buried_person = |imperial_grave = |historic_sites = Designated as a historic site by Komaki City |tangible_cultural_properties = Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror (Designated by Komaki City) |archaeological_site_number = |notable_features = |map = Japan Aichi |map = |type = |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = }}
'''Kōyashiki Kofun''' (甲屋敷古墳, ''Kōyashiki Kofun'') is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) from the [[Kofun period]], located in {{ill|Komaki|en|Komaki|WD=Q389537|lt=Komaki|1=Komaki}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}, Japan. It is part of the [[Koki Kofun Cluster]].
== Overview == The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|3rd century|en|3rd century|WD=Q8101|lt=3rd century|1=3rd century}} to the {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}}. Approximately two-thirds of the mound has been lost<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kōyashiki Kofun|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. During soil extraction work in the early {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era|1=Shōwa era}}, a large quantity of red {{ill|Pigment|en|Pigment|WD=Q161179|lt=Pigment|1=Pigment}} and a [[Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror]] were unearthed<ref name=":0" />. A portion of the burial chamber was discovered during a Komaki City historical survey in {{ill|1975|en|1975|WD=Q2479|lt=1975|1=1975}}<ref name=":0" />. On {{ill|March 25|en|March 25|WD=Q2454|lt=March 25|1=March 25}}, {{ill|1978|en|1978|WD=Q2483|lt=1978|1=1978}}, the triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror was Historic Site1 as a tangible {{ill|Cultural property|en|Cultural property|WD=Q2065736|lt=Cultural property|1=Cultural property}} by Komaki City<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun (on display at Komaki City History Museum)|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html|website=Komaki City Official Website|accessdate=2021-09-03|language=ja}}</ref>. On {{ill|August 15|en|August 15|WD=Q2774|lt=August 15|1=August 15}}, {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992|1=1992}} ([[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 4), the Kofun was Historic Site1 as a historic site by Komaki City<ref name=":0" />.
== burial goods == * Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror: Measuring 20.8 cm in diameter, it is believed to have been manufactured in Japan during the [[Kofun period]]<ref name=":1" />. The artifact is currently preserved and displayed at the [[Komaki City History Museum]]<ref name=":1" />. * Red pigment
== location == 2-474-1 Koki, [[Komaki City]], {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}
== Access == * A 3-minute walk from the Koki {{ill|Bus stop|en|Bus stop|WD=Q953806|lt=Bus stop|1=Bus stop}} served by {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|en|Meitetsu Bus|WD=Q6810397|lt=Meitetsu Bus|1=Meitetsu Bus}}.
== notes == {{notes}} <references />
== See Also == * [[Koki Kofun Cluster]] – A general term for the Kofuns in this area.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9126.html Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/4/9127.html Triangular-rimmed Shinjukyo mirror unearthed from Kōyashiki Kofun] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns]
{{translated page|ja|甲屋敷古墳|version=98203992|comment=Imported full ja history}}
